findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: '2 cfs 5 cfs 38 cfs' (3x); '2 cfs 31 cfs' (3x); '2 cfs 5 cfs 31 cfs' (3x).

findings.description: Content duplication: Each slide's content appears twice - once as embedded image with alt text summary, and again as extracted text (headings, bullet points). The presentation slides are image-based, so the HTML reproduces the content of each slide both as an image tag and as separate text elements, creating redundancy.
findings.evidence:
For example, Slide 2 content ('Eno River State Park Ecological Flow Study' with its bullet points) appears as an alt text AND as a separate

heading and
    list. This pattern repeats for all slides throughout the document.

findings.description: Repeated h2 headings with identical text. 'Eno River State Park Ecological Flow Study' appears as an h2 heading twice (pages 2 and 3 content), which could be confusing. The PDF had these as separate slides with the same title, but the HTML doesn't differentiate them.
findings.evidence:
Two consecutive sections both have '

Eno River State Park Ecological Flow Study

' with no distinguishing numbering or subtitle.

findings.description: Transect #7 cross-section profile graph is missing. The PDF shows transect photos for #7 (pages 18-19) but unlike all other transects, there is no corresponding cross-section elevation graph page for Transect #7.
findings.evidence: All other transects (#1-#6, #8-#12) have both photo slides and corresponding cross-section elevation graphs. Transect #7 only has photo slides. However, this appears to also be the case in the source PDF, so this is likely not a conversion error but a feature of the original document.
